summary: Focused verification of (a) login working for both users and (b) full Emergent branding removal. Backend logins return 200 with token+user for admin/5@Sohangso and dispatch/5@Grewal (NO 404). UI login flow works end-to-end: landing -> nav-portal-login -> /login -> /portal for admin, logout via 'Sign out' -> re-login as dispatch -> /portal. Browser title is 'Grewal Engineering Works' on landing, login and portal pages. No references to emergent-main.js, posthog, or 'Emergent | Fullstack App' anywhere in served HTML. Landing hero/gallery/footer sections render; /portal/dispatch and /portal/modules/packing load post-login. Full backend regression: 34 passed / 1 skipped (PDF extraction, optional).
action_items: User's original 404 was on their deployed custom domain (grewalengineeringworks.com) which is a stale build - user must redeploy from this env. Code is deployment-ready.

Minor (non-blocking): admin sidebar link for Settings does not use anchor href pattern '/portal/settings' - could not verify via selector alone. Dispatch sidebar (confirmed via screenshot) correctly shows NO Settings entry. Recommend adding data-testid='sidebar-settings-link' for future automated verification of role-based nav.
critical_code_review_comments: index.html is clean: correct title, inline SVG favicon, no external analytics/scripts. Good.
Login inputs use data-testid='login-username-input'/'login-password-input'/'login-submit-button' - consistent naming; no name/id fallback needed but present.
seed_data_creation: None (used pre-seeded users; pytest suite creates+cleans up its own TEST_ users, dispatch entries, packing slips)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Login form testids are login-username-input / login-password-input / login-submit-button (not ...-username/-password). Logout uses 'Sign out' text button in sidebar. Backend base URL from frontend/.env REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. All 34 backend tests pass in ~11s.
rca of the issue: User's reported 404 was against their production custom domain (grewalengineeringworks.com) which hosts a stale front-end/back-end build. Current preview env code returns 200 for both users and has no emergent/posthog artifacts. Fix: user must redeploy latest build; no code change needed.
